name: Manmohan Dogra
title: Tabla Virtuoso
tagline: Bridging Continents Through the Ancient Language of Rhythm
gharana: Banaras Gharana (with knowledge of Delhi & Ajrada)
quote: Playing the tabla is not just about mastering compositions but about developing a true sense of rhythm that can travel across traditions. Fusion is not about replacing one form with another; it is about blending them respectfully and finding a shared voice.

awards.name: Tabla Shree Award
awards.description: Awarded twice — one of the most coveted recognitions in Indian classical percussion, honouring exceptional mastery and contribution to the tabla tradition.

awards.name: Tabla Shiromani Award
awards.description: Recognising Manmohan as a 'crown jewel' of tabla artistry, this distinction celebrates his outstanding artistry, dedication, and service to Hindustani music.

awards.name: Critical Acclaim — Edinburgh Fringe
awards.description: Widely praised by critics for performances described as 'a rhythmic revelation' — technical precision fused with profound emotional depth.

performances.description: Performed as a solo tabla artist at the world's largest arts festival, receiving critical acclaim for his programme in Taal Teentaal alongside Edinburgh-based sitarist David Beards.
Returned to the Fringe with this ambitious concert, described by critics as a 'sonic tapestry' shifting between the meditative and the electrifying.

performances.type: Solo Concert — 'Rhythms of Tradition: A Tabla Journey'
performances.description: Presented a traditional tabla solo in Taal Teentaal at this prestigious cultural venue, showcasing the full breadth of the Banaras Gharana's classical repertoire.
Conducted educational workshops at universities across the UK, bringing Indian classical percussion to younger and wider audiences.
Pioneered cross-genre performances uniting the rhythmic bols of Indian classical Kathak dance with jazz, creating an original 'language of rhythm' for multicultural UK audiences.
In an ongoing creative partnership with Edinburgh-based sitarist David Beards, Manmohan redefines the tabla's role as an equal voice in rhythmic dialogue with the sitar.

artisticPhilosophy: What distinguishes Manmohan Dogra beyond his technical mastery is his capacity to make every performance a journey. Critics describe a 'quiet intensity' on stage — his improvisations unfold not as displays of virtuosity, but as narratives: each kaida a conversation, each rela an emotional arc. Manmohan is equally devoted to the future of his art as to its past, investing in the next generation through workshops in schools and universities across India and the UK.
